decision-tree.root-question: Is this Django project public-facing or shipping to a production environment with monitored uptime?

branch.conclusion: Skip this methodology. Keep stdlib logging with console output; ruff T20 still forbids print(). Prototype quality is sufficient.

branch.question: Does a log aggregator (Datadog, Loki, CloudWatch, Stackdriver) already ingest application logs?
Is the project deploying to a cloud platform whose logging tier ingests JSON natively (GCP Cloud Run, AWS Lambda, Fly.io)?

branch.conclusion: Install structlog only (no aggregator means Sentry is sufficient). Run rule r1 to wire JSON output for future ingestion; defer rule r2 until aggregator exists.
Full rollout. Apply rules r1 + r2 + r5. Cloud-native JSON ingestion makes structlog mandatory immediately.
Full rollout: rules r1 + r2 + r5. Aggregator already pays the JSON cost; Sentry pays for itself with one MTTR-reducing incident.

example.trace: prod-django + datadog → q1=yes → q2=yes → conclusion r2-sentry (full rollout)
